macro_summary: Stocks slipped on Monday as oil rose 2.3% to $104.26 after the UAE detected missiles from Iran, while the US Central Command denied a report of a warship strike. The S&P 500 eased 0.3% to 718.01, with the VIX jumping 7.7% to 18.29, reflecting caution ahead of heavy data and FOMC this week. Semis held up relatively well, with SOXX off just 0.6%.
commodities_note: WTI climbed 2.3% to $104.26 as the Hormuz crisis deepened with fresh missile activity. Gold fell 1.7% to $4,551, pressured by a firmer dollar (DXY +0.48%). The US 10-year yield rose 7bp to 4.45%, adding to rate-sensitive pressure on growth stocks.

singapore.summary: NIM compression enters day 48 with no relief from rates (US10Y 4.45%) or SGD strength (MAS steady). OCBC holds above 50-day while DBS struggles with momentum; UOB awaits Thursday's earnings to confirm if the worst of margin erosion is behind.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally bearish — dual-channel NIM squeeze intact
singapore.key_development: US10Y rose to 4.45%, reinforcing the rate headwind for SGD-denominated lending margins.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX slipped 0.6% to 462.06, but the cluster remains extremely extended — every mega-cap name (NVDA, AMD, INTC, MU, TSM, AVGO, LITE) is hard-blocked. Only LITE and AMD face near-term binary events; for the rest, a pullback of 10-20% is needed to reset R:R. ASML is the lone name below its 50-day, but its distorted R:R keeps it in CAUTION.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically overbought — no clean entries
semiconductors.key_development: MU's all-time high and AMD's -5.6% pre-earnings slip highlight the bifurcation within the cluster.

big_tech.summary: AMZN and GOOG remain extreme-extension CAUTION, while MSFT has slipped to HOLD after failing to hold its 50-day reclaimed level. PLTR reports tonight, with a 78x P/E demanding a flawless government AI growth beat. The capex boom remains structurally supportive, but entry points have evaporated.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — capex confirmed, but valuations and extensions block entry
big_tech.key_development: Google's custom AI chip push (per Motley Fool) adds a new competitive angle vs NVDA, but GOOG is still 20% above its 50-day.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: Second leg of NVDA's $2B AI optics partnership alongside LITE; photonics ramp benefits both.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if LITE's May 6 earnings confirm optics revenue accelerating above $1B quarterly, pulling COHR higher.

interconnected.name: SK Hynix
interconnected.reason: Primary HBM supplier to NVDA alongside MU; memory shortage narrative directly impacts revenue.
interconnected.entry_note: Add on a 15-20% pullback from recent highs if MU's earnings guide confirms HBM tightness through 2027.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC and data infrastructure silicon for hyperscalers — read-through from AVGO and AMZN capex.
interconnected.entry_note: Monitor for entry if AVGO's June 4 earnings or hyperscaler capex commentary signals accelerating custom silicon adoption.

active_risks: Iran-Hormuz crisis: UAE detected missiles from Iran; US Central Command denies warship strike. Oil at $104.26, Strait transit still severely disrupted.
US-China trade escalation: US proposes expanding Entity List to subsidiaries of sanctioned firms (Everstream Analytics, May 5), targeting further chip restrictions.
US rate hawkishness: 10Y yield jumps to 4.45%, keeping pressure on tech valuations and Singapore bank NIMs.
Semiconductor export controls tightening: new US guidelines on AI chip design software exports, affecting Cadence, Synopsys, and Chinese chipmakers.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil elevated near $104 keeps energy costs high and defense names (PLTR) in focus. Rate sensitivity penalizes extended growth stocks — GOOG, AMZN, LITE all extreme CAUTION. Singapore banks continue to suffer from wide US-SGD rate differential and MAS S$NEER strength.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: No new entries in extended semis or BigTech; wait for Hormuz de-escalation or a 10-15% pullback in key names. Singapore banks on hold until US10Y sustainably drops below 4.20%.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Thirteen of 16 names are hard-blocked on technical extension or distorted risk/reward; the rest are on hold ahead of earnings. No new money should be deployed until the Hormuz risk premium and rate-induced extension reset.
